recap: Confirmed: AI Lead Response System - Demo is live and working. Google Form -> Gemini (text prompt) -> Gmail send

top_mits.name: MIT 1 - Finish the Make Scenario
top_mits.description: Core flow works. Today = harden it (error handling, edge cases) and package it as a demo asset. ~30-45 min.
steps: Add basic error handling to each module
Tighten the Gemini prompt if needed
Name and describe the scenario clearly inside Make

top_mits.name: MIT 2 - Build the Proposal Template
top_mits.description: Turn Phase 3's offer ladder into an actual one-page document you can send to a real prospect. ~60-75 min.
steps: Open a blank doc (Google Docs, Notion, or Canva)
Build the 3-tier pricing block (Audit, Sprint, Retainer)
Insert today's demo as the proof element
Save it as a reusable template

top_mits.name: MIT 3 - Send Portfolio to 3 People
top_mits.description: Get outside feedback before a real client sees this. Needs MIT 1 finished first. ~30 min.
steps: Pick 3 specific people (small business owner, marketing sense, tech peer)
Prepare what you're actually sending (description, screen recording, 2-3 specific questions)
Log who you sent it to and when

secondary_tasks: 5 Outreach Messages to Real or Warm Prospects (Acquisition domain)
Draft One LinkedIn Post About Today's Build (Brand domain)
One Applied Skill Rep (Learning domain) - extend scenario with Slack/WhatsApp
Save/Organize Today's Assets in One Folder
Write Tomorrow's Single MIT Before Shutdown
what_not_to_do: Don't rebuild the Make scenario from scratch
Don't send the proposal template to a real prospect today
Don't pick 3 random people just to check the box
Don't start Phase 5's agent frameworks or LangChain
